name: Potential Security Software Discovery
notes: This rule triggers when a security software is discovered. Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of security software, configurations, defensive tools, and sensors that are installed on a system or in a cloud environment. This may include things such as firewall rules and anti-viruses.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude - Sysmon
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Security Software Discovery - Powershell, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Security Software Discovery - Linux, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude - Sysmon
TD_list: APPLY Potential Security Software Discovery on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Security Software Discovery - Powershell, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Security Software Discovery - Linux, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude - Sysmon
RED_list: Event name: Potential Security Software Discovery

Event description: Detects when a security software is discovered. Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of security software, configurations, defensive tools, and sensors that are installed on a system or in a cloud environment. This may include things such as firewall rules and anti-viruses.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
